title: Choose TUI library
description: Options: tty-prompt, cli-ui (Shopify), curses, reline. Need: hotkeys, text input, scrollable content, drill-down views. Research and pick one.
close_reason: Decision: cli-ui (same as Roast). Installed via Gemfile.

title: Design agent tools for queue access
description: What should agents be able to do with the queue? Read current item, add new items, mark complete. CLI commands vs MCP tools vs skill files?
close_reason: Deferred: Prototype uses in-memory queue in ReviewLoop. Agent queue access (MCP tools, CLI commands) can be added when we need persistent queues or multi-agent workflows.

title: Design the workflow DSL
description: Refine the Ruby DSL sketch for defining workflows. Key questions: source/context/analyze/on_approve/on_revise blocks - is this the right structure? How does it compare to Roast's DSL?
close_reason: Deferred: Current ReviewLoop class is simple enough without DSL. Add DSL when we need configurable workflows (different sources, custom analyze prompts, etc.)

title: Wire up review loop
description: Connect TUI + Claude: load diff hunks from ~/.dotfiles, analyze each, show response, handle approve/reject/revise actions, move to next item. In-memory queue only.
close_reason: ReviewLoop wires TUI + ClaudeClient + DiffParser: loads hunks from git, analyzes each with Claude, displays diff+analysis, handles a/r/c/v/q actions with session continuity for revisions

title: Define minimal prototype scope
description: What's the smallest thing that proves the concept? Probably: hardcoded items, single agent (no parallel), basic approve/reject/revise, no persistence. Spec this out.
close_reason: Scope defined. See EXPLORATION.md 'Prototype Scope (v0)' section.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
## Prototype Scope - v0

### Goal
Prove the UX feels right. After 10 minutes of use, you're in flow - not fighting the tool.

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Fast feedback loop (item → decide → next feels snappy)
- [ ] Right information density (enough context, not overwhelming)
- [ ] Revision flow feels natural (conversational)
- [ ] Want to keep using it

### In Scope
- Hardcoded list of items (3-5 strings to review)
- Real Claude agent (single, sequential - no parallelism)
- Basic TUI: show item + response, hotkeys for actions
- Actions: approve, reject, revise (free-form text)
- Session continuity for revise (agent remembers context)
- In-memory only (no persistence)

### Explicitly Cut
- JSONL queue file
- Workflow DSL
- Parallel/background agents
- Persistence across runs
- Drill-down views (diff, transcript)
- Custom context hooks

### Tech Stack (tentative)
- Ruby script
- CLI::UI or TTY gems for prompts
- Claude CLI for agent (--resume for sessions)

### Test Scenario
Review 3-5 code snippets. For each:
1. See snippet + Claude's analysis
2. Decide: approve / reject / revise
3. If revise: type feedback, see continued response
4. Move to next

### Open Question
What items should we hardcode for testing? Options:
- Code snippets with intentional issues
- Git diff hunks from a real repo
- Simple strings to keep it minimal

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
### Decision: Test Data
Use real git diff hunks from an actual repo. This matches the original motivating use case and proves the UX with realistic content.

Could use:
- Hunks from a repo with recent changes
- Or generate some test changes specifically for this

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
### Decision: Diff Source
Use /Users/derek/.dotfiles repo for real git diff hunks.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
### Test Data Confirmed
Source: /Users/derek/.dotfiles (HEAD~3..HEAD)

5 diff hunks available:
1. ghostty config - new file, 1 line
2. raycast package.json - removed 13 lines (background/menubar commands)
3. raycast types - removed 8 lines (corresponding type defs)
4. configure script - changed stow alacritty → stow ghostty
5. zshrc - simplified SPIN conditional (removed 5 lines)

Good variety: new file, deletions, small edits.

title: Build basic TUI shell
description: Ruby script with CLI::UI or TTY. Show diff hunk, display text, capture hotkey input (a/r/c/q). No agent yet - just the UI loop. Reference: EXPLORATION.md 'TUI Sketch' and 'Prototype Scope'
close_reason: Basic TUI shell with CLI::UI: diff display, suggestion text, hotkey input (a/r/c/q), review loop with summary

title: Decide: Roast extension vs standalone
description: Three options: extend Roast with queue/input cogs, build on top of Roast, or standalone with cherry-picked ideas. Queue-driven model is fundamentally different from Roast's step-execution model.
close_reason: Decision: Standalone for prototype. Clean separation, simpler to iterate. Can integrate with Roast later if queue model proves valuable.

title: Understand Claude CLI session mechanics
description: How does claude --resume work? Does it fit our sticky-session-per-item model? What's the session ID format? Can we fork sessions?
close_reason: Mechanics verified. See EXPLORATION.md 'Claude CLI Session Mechanics' section.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
## Findings: Claude CLI Session Mechanics

### Key Flags
- `--resume <session-id>` - Resume existing session by UUID
- `--fork-session` - When resuming, create NEW session ID (branch off)
- `--session-id <uuid>` - Use specific UUID for new session
- `-c, --continue` - Continue most recent session
- `--no-session-persistence` - Don't save to disk

### Session ID Format
- Standard UUID: `bf32670c-abf7-48d2-b5d2-7f29ae656aae`

### Getting Session ID
JSON output includes `session_id`:
```bash
echo "prompt" | claude -p --output-format json | jq -r '.session_id'
```

### Session Continuity (Verified)
```bash
# Create session
result=$(echo "Remember: 42" | claude -p --output-format json)
session=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.session_id')

# Resume it
echo "What number?" | claude -p --output-format json --resume "$session"
# Returns: 42
```

### Forking (Verified)
```bash
# Fork from base session - gets new ID, keeps context
echo "New prompt" | claude -p --output-format json --resume "$base" --fork-session
```
- Each fork gets unique session ID
- Context preserved from fork point
- Multiple forks from same base work independently

### Implications for Prototype
1. **Initial analysis**: Run prompt, capture session_id
2. **Revise flow**: Use `--resume <id> --fork-session` to continue with feedback
3. **Sticky sessions**: Store session_id per queue item
4. **No forking needed for simple revise**: Can just `--resume` without fork if we want linear conversation

### Open Question
Should revise fork (branch) or continue (linear)?
- Fork: Can try multiple revisions from same point
- Continue: Simpler, linear conversation per item

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
### Decision: Linear Continuation
Revise uses `--resume <id>` without fork. Simple linear conversation per item.

Forking available later if we want 'try multiple approaches' feature.

title: Pick a name for the tool
description: Candidates: review-loop, human-loop, hl, queue-review, agent-queue, signal, sift. Want something short, memorable, conveys human-in-the-loop concept.
close_reason: Decision: 'sift' - short, memorable, conveys filtering/reviewing concept. Renaming bin/roast-review to bin/sift.

title: Integrate Claude CLI for analysis
description: Call claude -p with diff hunk, capture response and session_id from JSON output. Reference: EXPLORATION.md 'Claude CLI Session Mechanics'
close_reason: ClaudeClient class: prompt(), analyze_diff(), session_id capture and resumption via --resume flag

title: Add session continuity for revise
description: When user types revision feedback, call claude --resume <session_id> with the feedback. Update stored session_id. Show new response. Reference: EXPLORATION.md 'Claude CLI Session Mechanics'
close_reason: Already implemented in ReviewLoop#revise_analysis: resumes session with --resume flag, updates stored session_id, shows revised analysis
